bridge_acceptance_criteria: Dangerous commands are rejected with validation_errors
Responses include stdout stderr exit_code timestamp and working_directory
Bridge status report includes command results summary
Tests cover allowed and rejected commands
Existing GIS and Bridge tests pass
bridge_instructions: Develop a controlled command-request service that lets ChatGPT request safe diagnostic commands through Bridge and receive detailed Codex responses. Support commands such as ls, grep/rg, git status, pytest, file head/tail, and structured repo inspections. Implement an allowlist and denylist. Reject dangerous shell forms including rm, curl to unknown domains, secrets exfiltration, credential reads, sudo, chmod recursive, network scans, package installs unless explicitly allowlisted, and arbitrary shell injection. Requests should enter through Bridge queue files and responses should publish back to Bridge status/report files. Add routine check behavior so Codex can periodically import Bridge queue, process safe commands, run model refresh when needed, publish latest status, and include detailed stdout/stderr/exit_code metadata. Do not execute unvalidated arbitrary shell commands. Do not expose secrets. Do not modify evidence outputs except through existing model refresh scripts.
bridge_title: Build safe Bridge command service for routine Codex checks
evidence_boundary: Arbitrary shell execution, secret exposure, or command output that cannot be traced to a validated request.
human_signal: We want to send practical inspection commands like grep and ls without breaking the evidence chain.
note: Build safe Bridge command service for routine Codex checks | Develop a controlled command-request service that lets ChatGPT request safe diagnostic commands through Bridge and receive detailed Codex responses. Support commands such as ls, grep/rg, git status, pytest, file head/tail, and structured repo inspections. Implement an allowlist and denylist. Reject dangerous shell forms including rm, curl to unknown domains, secrets exfiltration, credential reads, sudo, chmod recursive, network scans, package installs unless explicitly allowlisted, and arbitrary shell injection. Requests should enter through Bridge queue files and responses should publish back to Bridge status/report files. Add routine check behavior so Codex can periodically import Bridge queue, process safe commands, run model refresh when needed, publish latest status, and include detailed stdout/stderr/exit_code metadata. Do not execute unvalidated arbitrary shell commands. Do not expose secrets. Do not modify evidence outputs except through existing model refresh scripts.
shared_priority: Let us ask Codex for detailed repo observations while preserving safety and provenance.
shipping_goal: Safe Bridge command service with routine status publication every check cycle.
system_signal: A safe command layer turns Bridge from a queue into an auditable remote work interface.

Add reports/bridge_health_latest.md and .json summarizing queue counts, processed/rejected/blocked counts, latest routine check, latest Gist timestamp, command results, test status, scheduler_mode, ledger status, and top active tasks. Keep it concise and citation/link friendly. Update docs/BRIDGE_HEALTH_DASHBOARD.md.
bridge_title: Add Bridge health dashboard summary
evidence_boundary: Health report omits failed/rejected/blocked state.
human_signal: We need a fast way to know if the system is alive and what it is doing.
note: Add Bridge health dashboard summary | Create a compact health dashboard export for ChatGPT and humans. Add reports/bridge_health_latest.md and .json summarizing queue counts, processed/rejected/blocked counts, latest routine check, latest Gist timestamp, command results, test status, scheduler_mode, ledger status, and top active tasks. Keep it concise and citation/link friendly. Update docs/BRIDGE_HEALTH_DASHBOARD.md.
shared_priority: Give us one clean heartbeat view before issuing new tasks.
shipping_goal: Bridge health dashboard export.
system_signal: Multiple reports now exist and need aggregation for quick review.

bridge_instructions: Add safe command request examples that ChatGPT can place into Bridge queue. Include examples for ls, rg, head, tail, pytest focused tests, reading report summaries, and rejecting unsafe commands. Add docs/CHATGPT_COMMAND_REQUEST_EXAMPLES.md and templates/commands/*.json. Ensure examples include human_signal, system_signal, shared_priority, shipping_goal, evidence_boundary, and acceptance criteria.
bridge_title: Add command request examples for ChatGPT
evidence_boundary: Examples encourage unsafe or ambiguous shell behavior.
human_signal: I need to be able to ask Codex for repo visibility safely and consistently.
note: Add command request examples for ChatGPT | Add safe command request examples that ChatGPT can place into Bridge queue. Include examples for ls, rg, head, tail, pytest focused tests, reading report summaries, and rejecting unsafe commands. Add docs/CHATGPT_COMMAND_REQUEST_EXAMPLES.md and templates/commands/*.json. Ensure examples include human_signal, system_signal, shared_priority, shipping_goal, evidence_boundary, and acceptance criteria.
shared_priority: Make safe operational curiosity easy and unsafe requests obvious.
shipping_goal: Reusable command request examples for Bridge.
system_signal: The command service needs clear examples to reduce malformed requests.

bridge_instructions: Implement changelog automation that appends major Bridge/Codex changes after successful routine runs. Add scripts/update_changelog_from_bridge.py and CHANGELOG.md entries for Bridge status publishing, semantic propagation, command service, QSOF integration, evidence ledger, assessor packets, and routine scheduler. Include dates, commit references where available, test status, and artifact paths. Do not overwrite historical changelog entries.
bridge_title: Add changelog automation for Bridge cycles
evidence_boundary: Changelog overwrites history or omits validation status.
human_signal: We need the service history to stay readable as it evolves quickly.
note: Add changelog automation for Bridge cycles | Implement changelog automation that appends major Bridge/Codex changes after successful routine runs. Include dates, commit references where available, test status, and artifact paths. Do not overwrite historical changelog entries.
shared_priority: Keep the evolution legible, not just functional.
shipping_goal: Automated append-only Bridge changelog updates.
system_signal: Routine tasks are creating many artifacts and commits that need human-readable release notes.
